title: isonandra lanceolata f. angustata
intro: Isonandra lanceolata f. angustata, commonly known as a species of flowering plant, is a member of the family Sapotaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include various health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The therapeutic actions of Isonandra lanceolata f. angustata include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential use in treating wounds and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include various compounds such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

constituents: Isonandra lanceolata f. angustata has many active constituents, such as alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which possess various medicinal properties. These constituents include isonandrine, isonandrinone, and lanceolatin, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis, improve skin health, and prevent infections. The plant's constituents also include flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have been found to have antiviral and anti-cancer properties, potentially reducing the risk of diseases like HIV and certain types of cancer. parts: Isonandra lanceolata f. angustata has many medicinal parts, such as its roots and leaves, which are used in traditional medicine. The roots of Isonandra lanceolata f. angustata contain alkaloids like isonandrine and isonandrinine, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. preparations: Isonandra lanceolata f. angustata has many herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, which are used for various medicinal purposes. The tea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat conditions such as arthritis, while the tincture is used as a natural remedy for digestive issues, including nausea and indigestion. A cold infusion of the plant is sometimes used to treat fever and other systemic infections, and the essential oil extracted from the plant is used topically to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. side_effects: Isonandra lanceolata f. angustata can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea.
